source.description: Six-panel generated marker process sheet cropped into step frames and finished art.
source.notes: Honest backdated archive lesson; selected as a simple bold badge shape unlike recent food, face, vehicle, or cassette lessons.

process_strategy: The badge border, lightning bolt, rainbow bands, dots, and marker fills are introduced before the final panel so the finish only thickens and brightens existing parts.

final_elements.name: central lightning bolt
final_elements.description: A simple angular bolt inside the badge.

final_elements.name: rainbow bands and marker colors
final_elements.description: Three curved red, orange, and green rainbow bands behind the bolt.

final_elements.name: small accent dots
final_elements.description: Small dots around the badge interior.

final_elements.name: badge and bolt marker fills
final_elements.description: Yellow bolt, blue badge field, and colored dot fills.

frames.visible_job: Draws one rounded square sticker-badge outline with broad curved corners as the container for the bolt.
Adds the central lightning bolt inside the badge.
Adds and colors three curved rainbow bands behind the bolt.
Adds small accent dots around the existing bolt and colored rainbow.
Adds bright marker fills to the established badge, bolt, and dots, while leaving the rainbow colors already placed.

final_step.visible_job: Only retraces existing outlines, evens marker fills, and adds tiny highlights to already drawn shapes.

rejection_checks: No bolt, rainbow band, accent dot, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
